mechanism: As the effective interest rate on federal debt approaches nominal GDP growth, the debt dynamics become self-reinforcing. Each percentage point of r>g adds to the debt/GDP ratio, requiring larger primary surpluses to stabilize — surpluses that are politically unattainable.
regime_dependence: The relationship activates when r crosses g. Below this threshold, debt dynamics are self-stabilizing. Cross-country base rate: 35-40% fiscal stress within 10 years at r≈g, but dominated by non-reserve-currency issuers.
